title: [$250] iOS crash: Infinite onboarding navigation loop (Maximum update depth exceeded)

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem Users on iOS production are hitting an infinite navigation loop crash during the onboarding flow. The app crashes with "Maximum update depth exceeded" (React error). **Sentry issue:** https://expensify.sentry.io/issues/7332234716/ ### Symptoms - Rapid, repeated navigation to `OnboardingModalNavigator` visible in Sentry breadcrumbs - VL logs show `[OnboardingGuard] Redirecting to onboarding route` firing repeatedly with all boolean conditions as empty strings (serialized `false`/`undefined`) - Not reproducible locally — appears to require a specific timing/state combination ### Root Cause The `OnboardingGuard` in `src/libs/Navigation/guards/OnboardingGuard.ts` lacks idempotency. When `shouldSkipOnboarding` is `false`, the guard unconditionally returns a `REDIRECT` to the onboarding route — even when the user is **already on** the `OnboardingModalNavigator`. Each `REDIRECT` produces a `CommonActions.reset()` that changes the navigation state, which triggers: 1. `onStateChange` in `NavigationRoot.tsx` → Onyx writes (`updateOnboardingLastVisitedPath`) 2. Re-renders from Onyx state changes 3. Re-evaluation of the guard (via `useNavigationBuilder` calling `router.getStateForAction` during render) 4. Another `REDIRECT` → another reset → loop continues until React hits maximum update depth Additionally, `getOnboardingInitialPath()` (called by the guard to compute the redirect route) performs `Onyx.set()` side effects for certain user types (VSB, SMB, individual), which injects further state mutations into each loop iteration. ### Fix Add an idempotency check: if `OnboardingModalNavigator` is already present in `state.routes`, return `ALLOW` instead of issuing a redundant `REDIRECT`. This ensures the guard reaches a stable state after the first redirect. ## Platform - [x] iOS - [ ] Android - [ ] Web - [ ] Desktop - [x] Mobile Web
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @Krishna2323
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @Krishna2323

title: [$250] [Metrics] ManualSubmitToDestinationVisible — SubmitExpenseOrchestrator + centralized telemetry (part 1)

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Context This issue tracks **part 1** of the [ManualSubmitToDestinationVisible](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/83634) work (submit → destination visible telemetry and performance). It corresponds to the refactor delivered in [PR #87963](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87963). ## Goal Prepare and instrument the submit-to-destination flow **without changing intended product behavior**: consolidate orchestration and telemetry so follow-up changes (dismiss-first fast paths) can land safely. ## What shipped in #87963 - **`SubmitExpenseOrchestrator`** — Extracts the submit decision tree (including GPS permission handling and telemetry lifecycle) from `IOURequestStepConfirmation` into a dedicated render-prop component. Only the default **slow** path is active in this PR. - **Centralized telemetry in `submitFollowUpAction.ts`** — Single place for tracking session management (`startTracking`, `setFastPath`, `addOptimization`, `cancelTracking`), follow-up action state, and structured **`__DEV__`** logging (e.g. `[SubmitExpense] request_money_manual -> navigate_to_search (none) 105ms [slow path]`). - **`isTracking()`** — Replaces scattered `getSpan()` checks in navigation helpers and IOU actions where appropriate. - **`useEndSubmitNavigationSpans`** — Shared hook for span-ending logic previously duplicated in `SearchPageNarrow` and `SearchPageWide`. - **Bugfix** — Clear `trackingState` when `cancelSubmitFollowUpActionSpan` runs so telemetry state stays consistent. ## Out of scope (follow-up) - **Dismiss-first navigation fast paths** and **`[fast path]`** labeling are intentionally deferred to [PR #87768](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87768) (HOLD on #87963). ## Acceptance criteria - All expense submit flows behave as before (including distance + GPS permission). - Telemetry lifecycle is centralized; in dev, console shows structured `[SubmitExpense]` lines after submit. - No regressions in navigation after expense creation (Search, Inbox report, RHP, modal dismiss paths). ## Links - Parent epic: https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/83634 - Implementation PR: https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87963 - Follow-up PR: https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87768

title: [$250] Rename `Reports` page to `Spend`

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Weekly
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: NewFeature
labels.description: Something to build that is a new item.

body:
**Problem** When a workspace member navigates to the `Reports` page, if the page shows expenses, chats, search results, and accounting workflows alongside reports, then the page name no longer matches the content or navigation path, which makes the experience harder to understand and explain. **Solution** Rename the page from `Reports` to `Spend` so the destination better matches the financial workflows it contains. **Implementation details** - Rename the left navigation item from `Reports` to `Spend` - Rename the page header/title from `Reports` to `Spend` - Update the page icon to "receipts multiple" - No change to underlying report objects - No change to expense, chat, or accounting workflows - No change to permissions - No change to existing URLs and routes - No change to filtering, sorting, or search behavior - No redesign of the left nav information architecture beyond terminology updates required for this rename - No backend migration of core data models unless strictly necessary for display/copy consistency **Important note** This rename applies to the page/destination. It does not mean every use of the word `report` in the product should become `spend`. For example: - A page label like `Reports` should become `Spend` - An object like `reportID`, `expense report`, or internal model names should not be renamed unless explicitly required and separately scoped
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @situchan

title: [$250] Performance: remove Onyx selector from useArchivedReportsIdSet

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem `useArchivedReportsIdSet` used an Onyx `selector` to filter archived report IDs. On large accounts that produces a big derived value and triggers expensive `deepEqual` work on updates. ## Goal Drop the selector: compute the archived ID set in the hook body from the reports collection instead of passing a selector into `useOnyx`, avoiding redundant deep equality on large archived sets. ## Impact Author reported ~100ms improvement opening money request on iOS with ~2.1k archived reports (account-dependent). ## Implementation [#87899](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87899) — [No QA] Remove selector from `useArchivedReportsIdSet` ## Related (from PR) - [#80560](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/80560) - [#77173](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/77173) ## QA PR marked **No QA** — spot-check money request / flows using archived report filtering; no new console errors.

title: [$250] Performance: optimize LHN sidebar sorting with pre-computed sort keys

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem Left-hand navigation (LHN) sidebar sorting used `Intl.Collator.compare()` on **every** comparison in each sort pass — on the order of hundreds of thousands of calls per sort on large accounts. Each call applies locale-sensitive collation rules and is relatively expensive. ## Approach (PR) Pre-compute sort keys per report name: lowercase, zero-pad numeric segments, cache at module scope. Sort primarily compares plain strings; fall back to `Intl.Collator` only when sort keys tie (a small fraction of comparisons). **Tradeoff:** Locale-specific ordering for some diacritics (e.g. Swedish ä/ö, German ß, Spanish ñ) follows Unicode codepoint order instead of full locale collation. Numeric sorting is preserved. **Reported impact (author):** iOS ~740ms → ~164ms; Android ~2.1s → ~380ms on tested accounts. ## Implementation [#87847](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87847) — Optimize LHN sidebar sorting with pre-computed sort keys ## Related (from PR) - [#80560](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/80560) - [#77173](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/77173) ## QA / verification - Fresh/small account: create chats, pin one (stays on top), type in another (ordering updates as before). - High–chat-count account: sidebar still sorts sensibly; spot-check names with diacritics if relevant to your locale. - No new JS console errors.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @abzokhattab

title: [$250] Performance: simplify useAllPolicyExpenseChatReportActions (single pass, no heavy selector)

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem `useAllPolicyExpenseChatReportActions` used an Onyx `selector` that returned a large derived structure, which then went through `deepEqual` on updates. A second pass (`useMemo` / ID extraction) repeated work over the same data. ## Goal - Drop the heavy selector output so subscription updates are cheaper. - Merge the selector pass and ID/action lookup into **one** loop over reports, reading report actions by key directly. - Remove redundant `useMemo` / `useCallback` where no longer needed. - Remove the unused `policyExpenseChatReports` return value (no callers). Behavior should be unchanged; this is a refactor for fewer allocations and less work when opening expense flows. ## Impact Author reported ~150ms improvement when opening an expense (account-dependent). ## Implementation Merged in: [#87894](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87894) ## Related (from PR) - [#80560](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/80560) - [#77173](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/77173) ## QA / verification - Pure refactor / [No QA] on PR — confirm no JS console errors when opening expenses and using flows that depend on policy expense chat report actions.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @TMisiukiewicz

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug

body:
## Context `MoneyReportHeader` had grown to ~2400+ lines, mixing UI with business logic (hold menus, primary actions, expense actions, selection mode). That layout makes the header hard to maintain and can encourage unnecessary re-renders. ## Goal Split responsibility into focused hooks and components so the header stays thin and action logic lives next to the right Onyx/data access: - `useReportPrimaryAction` — primary action derivation; `getReportPrimaryAction` returns a plain value (no animation coupling) - `useExpenseActions` — secondary expense actions (hold, unhold, etc.); `isReportInSearch` passed in instead of hardcoded - `useTransactionThreadReport` — loads the transaction thread report internally via `useOnyx` - `ActionsBar` — renders subcomponents directly (no `ReactNode` props that force parent re-renders) - `MoneyReportHeaderHoldMenu` — extracted with an early return so the heavy menu tree is skipped when unused - Remove dead code (e.g. unused selection/payment refs and duplicated callbacks) Target: shrink `MoneyReportHeader` to a small shell (~300 lines) with behavior owned by the hooks above. ## Implementation Tracked in: [#87361](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87361) ## Related issues (from PR) - [#77173](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/77173) - [#84123](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/84123) ## QA / verification - Open an expense report (single transaction): **More** menu, each action (export sub-menu, Download PDF, etc.). - Search: multi-select expenses from the same report; selection dropdown shows correct actions. - No new JS console errors on these flows.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @TMisiukiewicz

title: [$250] Performance: reportAttributes recomputed all reports on transaction-only Onyx updates

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem When editing an expense (merchant, amount, description, etc.), the `reportAttributes` Onyx-derived value could recompute **all** reports in the collection (e.g. ~15k) instead of only the affected report(s). `dataToIterate` starts as `Object.keys(reports)` (full set). On the incremental path it was only narrowed when `updates.length > 0` (report / metadata / actions / nameValuePairs changes). When **only** a transaction update fired, `updates` stayed empty while `transactionsUpdates` was set — the incremental branch ran but `dataToIterate` was never scoped down, so transaction report IDs were merged into the full list and every report was recomputed. ## Expected outcome In the incremental update path, only reports that actually need recomputation should be processed (e.g. reset `dataToIterate` when entering that branch so transaction-driven updates do not retain the full key set). ## Impact On a high–report-count account, fixing this avoided unnecessary work; author measured ~800ms improvement when updating an expense on iOS (~15k reports). ## Implementation Merged in: [#87747](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87747) ## Related - PR: [#87747](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87747) — Fix `reportAttributes` recomputing all reports on transaction updates - Linked from PR: [#80560](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/80560) ## QA / verification - Edit an expense (e.g. amount); confirm values and UI stay correct. - No new JS console errors on that flow. - Spot-check high–traffic / many-reports account if possible.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @abzokhattab

body:
Part of https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/623020 # Problem A bad actor can work to gain control of an account from an unsuspecting user by adding the attacker's contact method as a secondary contact method, then setting that to the primary contact method (to receive future magic codes). ### Solution This isn't 100% fullproof, but at least is another step put in the path of the attacker to get through. This will be done in conjunction with some backend changes, but they do not need to be coordinated very tightly and this can be done first. - When setting a secondary contact method as primary - Prompt the user for a magic code (we have this same flow in many different places in the app, so you can use those for inspiration) - Send that code to the API for setting the new default contact method - It's fine to assume for now that these requests will always be successful, even if an invalid code is entered. Eventually, the backend will start verifying the codes and return an error message if it isn't successful
